title: Gold Holds Gain as US, Iran Prepare to Sign Interim Peace Deal
summary: Gold held gains, as the US and Iran prepared to sign an interim peace deal that may ease global inflationary pressures arising from the war.

title: Australian LNG Union Reaches Deal to End Strikes at Ichthys
summary: Unions in Australia and Japan’s Inpex Corp. agreed to end strikes at the Ichthys liquefied natural gas export facility after weeks of industrial action.

title: Oil Tankers U-Turn, Rush to Middle East Before Hormuz Reopening
summary: Two oil tankers heading toward Africa have u-turned in the Indian Ocean this week, switching their destinations to the Middle East as shipowners race to re-position vessels ahead of the possible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: PBOC Launches Tool to Boost Yuan Use by Other Central Banks
summary: China announced new money-market measures to broaden the use of its currency among entities like foreign central banks and sovereign wealth funds.

title: Iron Ore Sinks Back Below $100 as Abundant Supplies Hurt Outlook
summary: Iron ore sank below $100 a ton for the first time since March on signs of abundant seaborne supplies just as demand in China faces headwinds.

title: SNB Reviews Franc Alert Level Before Middle East Peace Deal
summary: The Swiss National Bank will this week reveal if it’s too soon to dial down its franc rhetoric before a prospective Middle East peace deal becomes reality.

title: ECB Officials Say Peace in Iran Isn’t Enough to Fix Energy Shock
summary: European Central Bank officials are signaling that a US-Iran peace accord won’t necessarily stop them lifting interest rates further, even if it prevents a more pronounced overshoot in inflation.

title: Bonds Rally Ahead of Fed as Oil Extends Slide: Markets Wrap
summary: Bonds climbed as oil headed for its longest losing streak in 10 months, with investors awaiting the Federal Reserve’s policy decision for clues on the outlook for global interest rates.

title: Glencore, Trafigura to Invest in Merdeka’s Hong Kong Listing
summary: Glencore Plc, Mercuria Energy Group and Trafigura Group are among key investors planning to buy securities in the Hong Kong listing of Indonesian gold miner PT Merdeka Gold Resources.

title: The Bad Gnus on SpaceX
summary: With a few giants destabilizing indexes, safety in numbers isn’t what it used to be.

title: China’s Regulator to Support Launch of Active ETF Products
summary: China’s regulator gave the greenlight for the rollout of actively managed exchange-traded funds, expanding the range of investment products available in the world’s second‑largest stock market and bringing it closer in line with more developed peers.

title: US Set to Offer Iran Broad Financial Gains in Peace Deal
summary: Iran is set to receive broad financial incentives as part of its agreement with the US, including the right to sell oil immediately, according to a final draft of the deal. Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar reports from Dubai. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Watchdog Urges More IPOs From AI, Hong Kong Listed Firms
summary: China’s top securities regulator pledged to welcome more domestic listings from artificial intelligence developers as well as Hong Kong traded firms to bolster onshore capital markets.

title: Oil Selloff Deepens as Iran Deal Spurs Brent’s Fifth Daily Loss
summary: Oil headed for the longest losing run in 10 months on gathering expectations that a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz will unleash a wave of supply, loosening the global crude market.

title: Qatar Moves LNG Ships Back to Mideast Ahead of Hormuz Reopening
summary: Qatar is beginning to bring some of its liquefied natural gas tankers back to the Middle East, as the major supplier prepares to ramp-up exports once the Strait of Hormuz reopens following a US-Iran deal.

title: Still See More Upside to Tech Trade: JPMorgan Asset Management
summary: Aisa Ogoshi of JPMorgan Asset Management says "risk obviously is in the tech trade" due to the market concentration. But she adds that "there's more upside to the tech trade" in terms of the AI data center supply chain. Ogoshi also discusses the outlook for the Bank of Japan. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BOJ Keeps Market Steady With Yen Volatility at Lowest Since 2021
summary: The Bank of Japan managed to keep financial markets relatively stable following its policy decision, leaving the yen in a narrow range that’s still historically weak.

title: Lingyi Seeks to Raise Up to $1.1 Billion in Hong Kong Listing
summary: China’s Lingyi iTech Guangdong Co. has started taking investor orders for a Hong Kong listing that may raise as much as HK$8.3 billion ($1.06 billion).

title: SpaceX Drew $800 Million From Korean Investors on Trading Debut
summary: South Korean retail investors snapped up almost $800 million of SpaceX shares on the stock’s first day of trading, underscoring strong demand for Elon Musk’s company after local investors were shut out from the world’s largest initial public offering.

title: Australia Must Gird for Shock-Prone Financial System, RBA Warns
summary: Australian institutions need to be ready to respond to a more shock-prone financial system as a strained geopolitical environment reshapes financial and economic linkages, a senior Reserve Bank official said Wednesday.

title: World Cup Exposes Growing Global Prediction Market Rift
summary: This year’s World Cup is the first since prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket exploded to popularity as a new way to bet on sports.

title: Brazil Firms Courted in Venezuela in Push for Stronger Ties
summary: Brazilian companies are racing to plant their flag in Venezuela as the country reemerges from more than a decade of isolation.

title: Bond Rally Fails to Allay Higher-for-Longer Global Rates Threat
summary: Governments around the world look to be facing lofty borrowing costs for at least the rest of the year, even as the fragile Middle East truce lowers energy prices and curbs inflationary fears.

title: Financial Details Emerge as US, Iran Prepare for Signing
summary: The US and Iran are preparing to formally sign an interim peace deal, with details of the accord still emerging and energy insiders skeptical of how fast it can reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg Economics' Chris Kennedy breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Stock Gauge Sinks as Traders Favor AI Winners Elsewhere
summary: Chinese stocks listed in Hong Kong are facing bleak milestones as a global rush into artificial intelligence supply chain players sidelines the Internet and consumer companies that dominate the offshore benchmark.

title: Laopu Gold’s Searing Rally Cools as Sales Slip, Bullion Falls
summary: Slowing sales, a slump in its share price and a selloff in the gold market are fueling concerns that Laopu Gold Co., one of China’s hottest consumer brands, is losing momentum.

title: Kalshi Aims to Expand Perpetual Futures After $5.5 Billion Debut
summary: Kalshi Inc. is looking to expand its new perpetual futures business beyond digital assets after the never-expiring derivatives racked up more than $5.5 billion of trading volume in their first two weeks on the prediction-market platform.

title: Inside the Palace: How Prabowo’s Circle Is Unnerving Investors
summary: Unpredictable state intervention under Indonesia’s president is turning the emerging-market darling into a global laggard.
